summary: eth-wallet-sentinel exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's lifecycle script (package.json line 30) and scanner.js together perform host reconnaissance and exfiltration to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js loads os, fs, https, and child_process; collects hostname (os.hostname()), user identity (execSync 'whoami'), filesystem state (fs.existsSync probes), and process environment, then POSTs to https://ddjidd564.github.io via https.request and uses curl as a fallback. The hardcoded GitHub Pages domain is not affiliated with any legitimate eth-wallet tooling publisher and is being used as a C2/exfil sink. The package.json install/lifecycle entry on line 30 stages the same exfiltration on install, so installing this package causes data leakage without any further user action. The package name suggests an Ethereum wallet utility, increasing the likelihood that targets are developers handling crypto-related secrets.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
